steps.title: Recall the equilateral height formula
steps.content:
For an equilateral triangle with side length $s$:
$$h = \dfrac{s\sqrt{3}}{2}$$
(This comes from a 30-60-90 split — the height is the long leg.)

steps.title: Solve for $s$
steps.content:
$$47\sqrt{3} = \dfrac{s\sqrt{3}}{2}$$
Multiply both sides by $2$ and divide by $\sqrt{3}$:
$$s = \dfrac{2 \cdot 47\sqrt{3}}{\sqrt{3}} = 94$$
That matches choice B — B is correct.
